topic: Stainless steel as an inert wine vessel

steps.label: cause
steps.text: Must or wine is held in a sound stainless-steel tank.

steps.label: mechanism
steps.text: Stainless steel does not contribute wood flavour, is readily cleaned and can be sealed and temperature controlled.

steps.label: effect
steps.text: The producer can manage fermentation or storage with little flavour contribution from the vessel and controlled oxygen exposure.

steps.label: exam_formulation
steps.text: Formative causal formulation: stainless steel is used as an inert, cleanable vessel that preserves the intended wine profile.

_meta.source_note: Inferred formative model - not official WSET assessment or mark-scheme content
